meta.title: Developer Productivity: Measuring the Modern Stack Impact
meta.excerpt: Quantified productivity improvements from adopting Next.js, TypeScript, and Strapi

props.text: A development agency was struggling with inconsistent project timelines and quality. Each project used different technologies, making it difficult to estimate accurately or reuse code. Developers spent weeks onboarding to each new project's tech stack.

props.title: Pre-Standardization State
props.description: 8 active projects with 4 different tech stacks

items.title: jQuery + PHP
items.description: 3 legacy client projects, no TypeScript, manual testing

items.title: React + Express
items.description: 2 projects, inconsistent patterns, no shared components

props.title: The Hidden Costs
props.body: Developers couldn't move between projects without ramp-up time. Project estimates were guesses, not data-driven. No shared component library meant rebuilding common UI repeatedly. No consistent testing strategy meant quality varied wildly.

props.text: We standardized on Next.js + TypeScript + Strapi with a shared component library and project templates, enabling code reuse and accurate estimation. All new projects use the standard stack, and legacy projects migrate during maintenance windows.

steps.title: Phase 1: Team Training
steps.description: 2-week intensive Next.js + TypeScript bootcamp for all developers

steps.title: Phase 2: Component Library
steps.description: Build shared UI component library, document patterns

steps.title: Phase 3: Starter Templates
steps.description: Create project templates for common use cases

steps.title: Phase 4: New Projects Only
steps.description: All new projects use standard stack, validate approach

steps.title: Phase 5: Legacy Migration
steps.description: Gradually migrate legacy projects during maintenance

props.title: Implementation Insights
props.body: Started with lowest-risk projects to validate the stack. Paired experienced developers with those learning new stack. Created internal documentation site using the same tech stack (dogfooding). Measured metrics from day one to prove ROI. Allowed exceptions for specific client requirements, but they required CTO approval.

stats.context: Faster delivery due to code reuse

props.title: Secondary Benefits
props.body: TypeScript caught 70% of bugs before runtime. Shared component library ensured consistent UX across all clients. Developer retention improved—team excited about modern stack. Hiring became easier—candidates attracted to modern tech. Sales won deals citing standardized approach and predictable delivery.

props.text: Stack standardization enables estimation accuracy, code reuse, and quality consistency. The upfront investment in training and tooling pays dividends in every subsequent project. Measure the metrics that matter: on-budget rate, code reuse percentage, onboarding time, bug escape rate. Standardization isn't about limiting choice—it's about optimizing for the 80% case and making exceptions explicit and deliberate.
